meta.generation_timestamp_policy: Do not store volatile timestamps in generated artefacts; use stable deterministic output for reviewable diffs.
meta.baseline_note: Uses the 120-card website Power Card set as the operational baseline; the previous 80-card realistic generated set remains historical validation evidence.

levels.name: Encounter
levels.child_facing_summary: Recognise it and use it safely.
levels.facilitator_definition: Recognise parts, signals, inputs, materials, expected behaviour, and safe setup before trying to control or alter them.

dimensions.adult_support: Adult chooses safe parts, sets boundaries, and models observation language.
dimensions.child_independence: Child recognises, sorts, points, describes, and handles parts within prepared choices.
dimensions.kit_state: Loose sample parts, unplugged demos, or safely preassembled examples; no child-made circuit or mechanism required.
dimensions.physical_build_complexity: No construction beyond handling, sorting, placing, or attaching obvious parts.
dimensions.debugging_evidence_expectation: Notice and name what happens; no systematic debugging required.
dimensions.integration_readiness: Not integration-ready; builds vocabulary and safe familiarity for later Power Cards.

levels.name: Activate
levels.facilitator_definition: Make one simple output, input, sequence, message, test, or design move work in a controlled setup.
levels.coding_expectation: No child-authored code; preloaded or plug-and-play code is allowed.

dimensions.adult_support: Adult provides the setup or wiring route and supervises safe operation.
dimensions.child_independence: Child follows a known setup to make one basic thing happen.
dimensions.kit_state: Preselected parts, direct wiring, preloaded board, servo tester, template, or plug-and-play module.
dimensions.coding_expectation: No child-authored code; preloaded or plug-and-play code is allowed.
dimensions.physical_build_complexity: One actuator, input, sensor, join, material, message, or test setup with minimal assembly.
dimensions.debugging_evidence_expectation: Check whether the expected basic output/input appears; use simple yes/no evidence.
dimensions.integration_readiness: Can support a very simple Integration Card when paired with another Level 2 power.

levels.name: Adjust
levels.facilitator_definition: Change one variable such as speed, range, threshold, timing, mapping, material fit, test method, or criterion.
levels.coding_expectation: Adjust one parameter, setting, threshold, timing value, or mode in a known pattern.

dimensions.adult_support: Adult gives a known working setup and helps isolate the one variable to change.
dimensions.child_independence: Child changes one setting, parameter, threshold, timing value, mode, material property, or mapping and observes the result.
dimensions.kit_state: Working starter setup with one adjustable element.
dimensions.coding_expectation: Adjust one parameter, setting, threshold, timing value, or mode in a known pattern.
dimensions.physical_build_complexity: One subsystem with one controlled variation; comparison between before and after is expected.
dimensions.debugging_evidence_expectation: Record or explain how the single change affected behaviour.
dimensions.integration_readiness: Ready to feed a controlled variable into a simple combined challenge.

levels.name: Apply
levels.child_facing_summary: Use it in a new context.
levels.facilitator_definition: Transfer a known skill to a different build, user, material, environment, subsystem, or constraint.
levels.coding_expectation: Guided adaptation of a known code or logic pattern.

dimensions.adult_support: Adult provides a familiar pattern and prompts transfer to a new context.
dimensions.child_independence: Child adapts a known approach for a different user, material, environment, mechanism, message, or constraint.
dimensions.kit_state: Starter pattern plus a new physical context or constraint.
dimensions.coding_expectation: Guided adaptation of a known code or logic pattern.
dimensions.physical_build_complexity: One subsystem transferred into a new build context, with fit, mounting, access, or use constraints.
dimensions.debugging_evidence_expectation: Test whether the known pattern still works in the new context and name needed changes.
dimensions.integration_readiness: Ready for Integration Cards that depend on one adapted subsystem.

levels.name: Coordinate
levels.facilitator_definition: Coordinate multiple related elements inside the same primary family, while keeping other families supporting rather than primary.
levels.coding_expectation: Coordinate multiple inputs, outputs, states, messages, or subsystems.

dimensions.adult_support: Adult helps plan order, interfaces, and conflict points across parts.
dimensions.child_independence: Child coordinates multiple elements so each has a clear role.
dimensions.kit_state: Multiple prepared subsystems, controls, sensors, states, messages, moving parts, structural elements, or material choices.
dimensions.coding_expectation: Coordinate multiple inputs, outputs, states, messages, or subsystems.
dimensions.physical_build_complexity: Multiple elements must work together without collision, conflict, overload, confusion, or unclear sequencing.
dimensions.debugging_evidence_expectation: Use trials or a simple checklist to show the coordinated system works as intended.
dimensions.integration_readiness: Strong preparation for Integration Cards; may be ready to combine across families.

levels.name: Improve
levels.facilitator_definition: Debug, refine, optimise, or explain trade-offs in the family object of study; only make Debugging/Testing primary when the diagnostic method is the learning target.
levels.coding_expectation: Debug, optimise, compare, or justify trade-offs in code, logic, or physical behaviour.

dimensions.adult_support: Adult supports fair testing, safety limits, and trade-off discussion.
dimensions.child_independence: Child identifies a weakness, chooses a fix or comparison, and justifies the trade-off.
dimensions.kit_state: Existing working or partly working build with evidence to compare before and after.
dimensions.coding_expectation: Debug, optimise, compare, or justify trade-offs in code, logic, or physical behaviour.
dimensions.physical_build_complexity: Refinement can involve mechanism, structure, power, materials, controls, sensing, communication, logic, or user fit, but the primary family remains the learning focus.
dimensions.debugging_evidence_expectation: Use before/after evidence, repeated trials, measurements, or user feedback to justify improvement.
dimensions.integration_readiness: Ready for more demanding Integration or Invention Cards when safety and support needs are clear.

power_cards.title: Recognise actuators
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can recognise motors, servos, and solenoids and describe their motion.

required_assets.preparation_notes: Unwired sample for recognising rotation.
Unpowered sample for recognising angle motion.
Adult-prepared push-pull demo sample; child observes only.

power_cards.success_condition: Actuator types are matched to rotation, angle, or push-pull motion.
debug_prompts: What part moves?
Does it spin, turn to a position, or push?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Find a real object that uses each motion.
power_cards.result_alt: Recognise actuators result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Spin a DC motor
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can make a DC motor spin safely.

required_assets.preparation_notes: Ready for direct low-voltage motor test.
Adult inserts cells and checks the pack is switched off before handout.
Use short leads with intact insulation.

power_cards.success_condition: Motor spins while wires and fingers stay safe.
debug_prompts: Is there a complete power path?
Is the shaft free to turn?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Reverse the leads and observe direction.
power_cards.result_alt: Spin a DC motor result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Set a servo angle
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can make a servo move to a chosen angle.

required_assets.preparation_notes: Servo is ready to plug into the labelled breakout pins.
Loose horn for marking visible positions.
Board is flashed before session; children select positions but do not edit code.
Adult attaches board and labels servo pins before session.

power_cards.success_condition: Servo moves to at least two chosen positions.
debug_prompts: Is the signal wire in the right place?
Is the horn blocked?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Mark three useful angles for a puppet or gate.
power_cards.result_alt: Set a servo angle result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Adjust speed with gearing
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can use gearing or wheel size to change speed and force.
power_cards.success_condition: Two setups produce visibly different speed or torque.
debug_prompts: Which gear is driving?
Is anything slipping?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Say which setup is faster and which setup pulls harder.
power_cards.result_alt: Adjust speed with gearing result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Move a linkage
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can make a linkage turn one motion into another.
power_cards.success_condition: Linkage repeats a predictable motion without jamming.
debug_prompts: Where is the pivot?
Is the link too tight?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Change the pivot position and compare travel.
power_cards.result_alt: Move a linkage result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Coordinate two actuators
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can coordinate two moving parts so they do not collide.
power_cards.success_condition: Two actuators complete a repeated movement without collision.
debug_prompts: Which part must move first?
Where could the parts hit?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Add a pause or offset to make the motion smoother.
power_cards.result_alt: Coordinate two actuators result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Improve unreliable motion
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can refine a moving mechanism that sticks, slips, or overshoots.
power_cards.success_condition: Failure rate drops after a named mechanical change.
debug_prompts: Is the problem friction, alignment, power, or timing?
What evidence shows improvement?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Compare two fixes and explain the trade-off.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is refining the mechanism motion; debugging evidence supports the Movement improvement.
power_cards.result_alt: Improve unreliable motion result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Explain motion trade-offs
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can explain a trade-off between speed, force, range, and reliability.
power_cards.success_condition: Explanation links a design choice to a measured movement result.
debug_prompts: What improved?
What got worse?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Recommend one setup for a new challenge.
power_cards.result_alt: Explain motion trade-offs result build on a workshop table

Adult inserts cells and checks switch state before handout.
Switch is ready to place in the simple circuit.
Use short leads with intact insulation.

power_cards.success_condition: The LEDs light without warm wires or reversed polarity.
debug_prompts: Is positive connected to positive?
Is the strip rated for this pack?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Add a switch so the strip can be turned off easily.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is powering one safe load.
power_cards.result_alt: Light an LED strip safely result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Measure battery drop
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can notice when a battery voltage drops under load.
power_cards.success_condition: A measurement shows how voltage changes when the motor runs.
debug_prompts: Are the meter leads in the right sockets?
Does the reading change only when the load runs?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Compare a fresh pack and a tired pack.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is measuring safe supplied power.
power_cards.result_alt: Measure battery drop result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Choose a pack for a motor
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can choose a power pack that suits a motor and its job.
power_cards.success_condition: The chosen pack runs the motor reliably without unsafe heat or strain.
debug_prompts: Does the motor start under load?
Do any parts get warm?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Explain why the other pack was not chosen.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is applying power choice to a real load.
power_cards.result_alt: Choose a pack for a motor result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Pick a material for a job
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can choose a material because it bends, holds, slides, or protects.
power_cards.success_condition: A material is chosen for a named property and job.
debug_prompts: What does this part need to do?
Should it bend, stay stiff, grip, or slide?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Find a second material that could do the job differently.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is recognising material properties for making.
power_cards.result_alt: Pick a material for a job result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Make a clean cardboard hinge
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can cut, fold, and tape a cardboard hinge that opens smoothly.
power_cards.success_condition: The hinge opens and closes ten times without tearing.
debug_prompts: Is the fold scored in the right place?
Is the tape stopping the hinge from moving?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Try a fabric or paper hinge and compare it.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is fabricating a simple moving joint.
power_cards.result_alt: Make a clean cardboard hinge result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Make two matching parts
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can use a template to make two parts the same size.
power_cards.success_condition: Two parts line up closely enough to assemble as a pair.
debug_prompts: Did the template move?
Which edge is the reference edge?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Make four matching parts and stack-check them.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is improving fabrication repeatability.
power_cards.result_alt: Make two matching parts result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Assemble a removable cover
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can make a cover that protects a build but can be opened for repair.
power_cards.success_condition: The cover stays on during use and can be removed without tearing the build.
debug_prompts: Where does access need to happen?
What part should be permanent and what part should open?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Add a cable route or battery door.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is coordinating parts and assemblies for access.
power_cards.result_alt: Assemble a removable cover result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Put action steps in order
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can arrange start, action, wait, and stop steps in a sensible order.
power_cards.success_condition: The sequence can be acted out from start to finish without missing a step.
debug_prompts: What has to happen first?
Where does the action stop?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Add a repeat step and act it out again.
power_cards.result_alt: Put action steps in order result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Make a timer sequence
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can make an output turn on, wait, then turn off.
power_cards.success_condition: The output follows the on-wait-off order three times.
debug_prompts: Which step is happening now?
Is the wait too short to notice?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Change the wait time and compare the behaviour.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is activating a simple sequence.
power_cards.result_alt: Make a timer sequence result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Use if-then in a prop
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can make a prop do one action if a condition is true.
power_cards.success_condition: The prop acts only when the chosen condition is met.
debug_prompts: What is the if part?
What should happen when it is not true?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Add a different action for the else case.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is applying logic to a real object.
power_cards.result_alt: Use if-then in a prop result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Coordinate start, wait, stop
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can coordinate several timed steps so a mini machine behaves predictably.
power_cards.success_condition: The machine follows the same timed pattern for three runs.
debug_prompts: Which step overlaps?
Where does the timing need a pause?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Add a second wait to make the action clearer.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is coordinating related timed actions.
power_cards.result_alt: Coordinate start, wait, stop result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Match signals to messages
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can match a light, sound, movement, or radio signal to a simple message.
power_cards.success_condition: Signals are matched to messages such as ready, stop, found, or help.
debug_prompts: Who needs to notice the message?
Can the message be seen or heard from far enough away?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Choose a signal for a noisy room and explain why.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is recognising communication channels.
power_cards.result_alt: Match signals to messages result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Make an alert pattern
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can make a light or sound pattern that means one clear thing.
power_cards.success_condition: Another person can tell which alert pattern means the chosen message.
debug_prompts: Is the pattern too fast?
Does it look different from the other signal?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Create a second pattern for a different message.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is adjusting a signal to carry meaning.
power_cards.result_alt: Make an alert pattern result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Send a message across a table
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can send a simple message from one model to another nearby model.
power_cards.success_condition: A receiver reacts when the sender sends the chosen message.
debug_prompts: Are sender and receiver using the same channel?
Is the receiver powered and listening?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Try one nearby and one slightly farther position and notice whether the message still arrives.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is applying communication between objects.
power_cards.result_alt: Send a message across a table result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Coordinate sender and receiver
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can make a sender and receiver share clear roles in a linked build.
power_cards.success_condition: Each side has a clear job and the linked action works repeatedly.
debug_prompts: Which side starts the message?
What should the receiver show back?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Add an acknowledgement signal.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is coordinating communication roles.
power_cards.result_alt: Coordinate sender and receiver result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Name the symptom
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can describe what a broken build is doing before guessing why.
power_cards.success_condition: The symptom is described as an observation, not a guess.
debug_prompts: What do you see or hear?
What changed from the working example?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Write two possible causes after naming the symptom.
power_cards.result_alt: Name the symptom result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Test one connection
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can test one connection before changing the whole build.
power_cards.success_condition: One connection is confirmed working or not working with a simple test.
debug_prompts: What are you testing right now?
What result would prove the connection works?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Mark the tested connection and move to the next one.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is a focused test; power parts make the test visible.
power_cards.result_alt: Test one connection result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Change one thing at a time
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can change one part of a build and compare the result.
power_cards.success_condition: The result is linked to one named change.
debug_prompts: What stayed the same?
What changed between the two tests?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Run the original test again to confirm the difference.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is controlled comparison.
power_cards.result_alt: Change one thing at a time result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Build a repeatable test rig
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can make a simple test rig that gives comparable results each time.
power_cards.success_condition: Three test runs can be compared because the setup stays the same.
debug_prompts: What part of the test must not move?
How will you record the result?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Use the rig to compare two prototypes fairly.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is coordinating evidence collection with a repeatable setup.
power_cards.result_alt: Build a repeatable test rig result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Choose a user and job
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can name who a build is for and what job it should help with.
power_cards.success_condition: A user and job are named before choosing a solution.
debug_prompts: Who will use it?
What are they trying to do?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Name one thing that would make the job easier or harder.
power_cards.result_alt: Choose a user and job result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Sketch two quick ideas
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can sketch two different ideas before choosing one to build.
power_cards.success_condition: Two ideas are visibly different and one is chosen for a reason.
debug_prompts: What is different about idea two?
Which one is easier to test quickly?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Circle one promising feature in each sketch before choosing what to test.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is making design choices visible before building.
power_cards.result_alt: Sketch two quick ideas result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Test comfort with a handle
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can test whether a handle, button, or grip is comfortable to use.
power_cards.success_condition: Feedback leads to one change in size, position, or shape.
debug_prompts: Where did the user hesitate?
What felt awkward to hold or press?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Compare comfort for two different hand sizes.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is using feedback to adjust a human-facing feature.
power_cards.result_alt: Test comfort with a handle result build on a workshop table

power_cards.title: Make a prototype fit a real place
power_cards.i_can_statement: I can adapt a prototype so it fits the space where it will be used.
power_cards.success_condition: The prototype fits the chosen space and still performs its job.
debug_prompts: What is the tightest space?
What changed when the size changed?
power_cards.stretch_challenge: Make a smaller version without losing the main function.
power_cards.classification_rationale: Primary learning is applying design constraints to a real context.
power_cards.result_alt: Make a prototype fit a real place result build on a workshop table

integration_cards.title: Button-controlled motor
integration_cards.challenge: Make a motor run only while a button is pressed.
integration_cards.success_condition: Motor starts and stops with the button in five trials.
make_it_yours_prompts: Turn it into a fan, wheel, or creature motion.

integration_cards.title: Light-sensitive sign
integration_cards.challenge: Make a sign that lights differently when covered or uncovered.
integration_cards.success_condition: Light response changes at the intended threshold.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it a night light, secret message, or warning sign.

integration_cards.title: Automatic door
integration_cards.challenge: Build a door that opens when a visitor is detected.
integration_cards.success_condition: Door opens and closes without jamming in repeated trials.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it for a shop, castle, or pet shelter.

integration_cards.title: Traffic lights
integration_cards.challenge: Build a traffic light model with a timed sequence.
integration_cards.success_condition: Lights change in a repeatable safe order.
make_it_yours_prompts: Adapt it for a road, train crossing, or hallway.

integration_cards.title: Tilt-controlled game
integration_cards.challenge: Make a game that responds to tilt or direction input.
integration_cards.success_condition: Player input changes game state in a consistent way.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it a maze, balance game, or reaction challenge.

integration_cards.title: Sensor-controlled robot
integration_cards.challenge: Build a robot that changes movement based on a sensor.
integration_cards.success_condition: Robot reacts to the target condition without manual control.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it avoid walls, follow light, or guard a boundary.

integration_cards.title: Remote-controlled vehicle
integration_cards.challenge: Build a vehicle that responds to remote commands.
integration_cards.success_condition: Vehicle responds to forward, turn, and stop commands reliably.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it a rover, rescue cart, or parade float.

integration_cards.title: Servo-powered puppet
integration_cards.challenge: Build a puppet with one expressive servo-powered motion.
integration_cards.success_condition: Motion supports the puppet character without tearing the material.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it wave, blink, bow, or snap.

integration_cards.title: Alarm system
integration_cards.challenge: Build an alarm that detects a condition and sends or shows an alert.
integration_cards.success_condition: Alarm triggers only for the chosen condition.
make_it_yours_prompts: Protect a treasure box, pet door, or model museum.

integration_cards.title: Robotic arm
integration_cards.challenge: Build a simple arm with controlled joint motion.
integration_cards.success_condition: Arm moves through the planned range without collapsing or colliding.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it lift, point, stamp, or sort.

integration_cards.title: Hinged bridge
integration_cards.challenge: Build a hinged structure that opens and closes while carrying a small load.
integration_cards.success_condition: Hinge moves and the bridge supports the target load.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it a drawbridge, flap, or folding stage.

integration_cards.title: Battery test station
integration_cards.challenge: Create a station for safely choosing and checking a battery for a load.
integration_cards.success_condition: A user can choose a suitable battery and reject an unsafe option.
make_it_yours_prompts: Theme it as a garage, lab, or mission control desk.

integration_cards.title: Chassis improvement clinic
integration_cards.challenge: Improve a weak chassis using a repeatable test.
integration_cards.success_condition: The same test shows a better result after the redesign.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it survive a ramp, bump, or carrying task.

integration_cards.title: Multiplayer radio tag game
integration_cards.challenge: Build a radio game where two players send commands or scores.
integration_cards.success_condition: Two players can complete one round with clear message rules.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it cooperative, competitive, or hidden-role.

integration_cards.title: Data beacon sculpture
integration_cards.challenge: Make a sculpture that senses one condition and sends a simple message.
integration_cards.success_condition: Message matches the sensed condition and the sculpture stays intact.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it a weather creature, mood object, or secret beacon.

integration_cards.title: Safe motor driver demo
integration_cards.challenge: Control a motor through a protected power path.
integration_cards.success_condition: Motor control works and the risky current path is protected.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it a lab demo for younger makers.

integration_cards.title: Calibrated greenhouse window
integration_cards.challenge: Open a model greenhouse window when conditions cross a calibrated threshold.
integration_cards.success_condition: Window opens and closes at sensible conditions without chatter.
make_it_yours_prompts: Grow imaginary desert, rainforest, or kitchen herbs.

integration_cards.title: Fabricated control panel
integration_cards.challenge: Build a usable mounted control panel.
integration_cards.success_condition: A peer can operate the panel and the controls stay mounted.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it a cockpit, DJ board, or factory console.

integration_cards.title: Range test course
integration_cards.challenge: Create a fair test course for unreliable wireless messages.
integration_cards.success_condition: The same course can compare two communication setups.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it a field trial, spy mission, or sports drill.

integration_cards.title: Weight-saving rover frame
integration_cards.challenge: Reduce rover frame weight while keeping movement reliable.
integration_cards.success_condition: Rover still moves and carries the required load after weight reduction.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it fast, strong, or long-running.

integration_cards.title: Timed launch gate
integration_cards.challenge: Build a gate that opens after a timed countdown.
integration_cards.success_condition: Gate releases at the intended time without snagging.
make_it_yours_prompts: Use it for a race, marble run, or stage reveal.

integration_cards.title: Sound alert treasure box
integration_cards.challenge: Build a box that notices opening and sends or plays an alert.
integration_cards.success_condition: Alert triggers on opening and stays quiet when closed.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it magical, scientific, or spy-themed.

integration_cards.title: Joystick crane
integration_cards.challenge: Control a simple crane with joystick directions.
integration_cards.success_condition: Crane moves in intended directions without tipping.
make_it_yours_prompts: Lift cargo, rescue objects, or stack blocks.

integration_cards.title: Repair a buggy circuit game
integration_cards.challenge: Create or solve a circuit fault using evidence rather than guessing.
integration_cards.success_condition: Fault is found, explained, fixed, and retested.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it a timed rescue or mystery challenge.

integration_cards.title: Choose-and-build enclosure
integration_cards.challenge: Design and fabricate an enclosure for a specific prototype.
integration_cards.success_condition: Enclosure meets chosen criteria and protects or exposes the right parts.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it wearable, tabletop, portable, or display-ready.

integration_cards.title: Sensor fusion maze cart
integration_cards.challenge: Build a cart that uses two sensor cues to navigate a maze condition.
integration_cards.success_condition: Cart makes better decisions with two sensors than one.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it cautious, speedy, or curious.

integration_cards.title: Radio scoring display
integration_cards.challenge: Show remote score messages on a powered display.
integration_cards.success_condition: Display updates only for valid score messages.
make_it_yours_prompts: Use it for quiz, racing, or team challenge scoring.

integration_cards.title: Servo sign with visitor input
integration_cards.challenge: Make a sign that changes position when a visitor presses a control.
integration_cards.success_condition: The sign moves to the intended position and survives repeated presses.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it welcome, warn, vote, or reveal.

integration_cards.title: Low-power night light
integration_cards.challenge: Build a night light that uses power carefully and responds to darkness.
integration_cards.success_condition: Light turns on in darkness and the power choice is justified.
make_it_yours_prompts: Make it calm, colourful, or creature-shaped.

integration_cards.title: Iteration sprint for weak robot
integration_cards.challenge: Improve a robot that moves poorly because of interacting mechanical and structural faults.
integration_cards.success_condition: A repeated test shows improvement and the team explains what trade-off remains.
make_it_yours_prompts: Optimize it for speed, pushing, reliability, or style.

invention_cards.open_ended_challenge: Invent a playground feature that a child with a chosen need could use or enjoy.
constraints: must name the user need
must include a test for stability or usability
reflection_prompts: Which design choice served the user most?
What trade-off did the team accept?

invention_cards.open_ended_challenge: Invent an exhibit that notices visitor behaviour and responds without needing a facilitator.
constraints: must avoid constant false alarms
must make the response understandable
reflection_prompts: What counted as a real trigger?
How did you reduce false triggers?

invention_cards.open_ended_challenge: Invent a rover or vehicle that can complete a rescue task in a model environment.
constraints: must carry or move a target object
must complete a repeatable course test
reflection_prompts: What subsystem limited the rover most?
What evidence shows the improvement helped?

invention_cards.open_ended_challenge: Invent a model habitat that reacts to pet needs such as light, door access, or temperature.
constraints: must define the pet need
must include one automatic response
reflection_prompts: What condition did the habitat sense?
Could the response be unsafe or annoying?

invention_cards.open_ended_challenge: Invent a game where players send, receive, or interpret messages at a distance.
constraints: must support at least two players or roles
must have a clear rule for ambiguous messages
reflection_prompts: What made the game fair?
What message rule was hardest to explain?

invention_cards.open_ended_challenge: Invent a kitchen-helper model for someone with limited reach, grip, or attention.
constraints: must choose one user limitation
must avoid sharp or food-contact hazards
reflection_prompts: How did the prototype reduce effort?
What safety issue remains?

invention_cards.open_ended_challenge: Invent a toy that changes behaviour to save or explain energy use.
constraints: must justify the power choice
must show or explain an energy-saving behaviour
reflection_prompts: What uses the most power?
What did the energy-saving choice make less fun or less capable?

invention_cards.open_ended_challenge: Invent an object that senses a weather-like condition and tells a story or signal from it.
constraints: must sense one condition
must make the output meaningful to a viewer
reflection_prompts: How did the form support the message?
What could cause a misleading reading?

invention_cards.open_ended_challenge: Invent a model factory step that sorts, moves, or signals items.
constraints: must define what counts as a sorted item
must run at least five repeated trials
reflection_prompts: What caused sorting errors?
Which improvement changed the success rate?

invention_cards.open_ended_challenge: Invent a way for club members to send a simple secret or coded message.
constraints: must define sender and receiver roles
must include one way to avoid accidental messages
reflection_prompts: What made the code understandable to friends but not everyone?
What happens when the wrong message is received?

invention_cards.open_ended_challenge: Invent a stage prop that changes, reveals, or moves in response to a performer.
constraints: must survive repeated performance use
must not require hidden facilitator intervention
reflection_prompts: What performer action controls it?
What broke or drifted after repeats?

invention_cards.open_ended_challenge: Invent a model or tool that helps makers notice a safe setup or unsafe condition.
constraints: must focus on one safety risk
must explain without shaming the user
reflection_prompts: What risk did it make visible?
How could the warning be misunderstood?

invention_cards.open_ended_challenge: Invent a creature that uses motion, sensing, or messages to seem to have a personality.
constraints: must show at least two behaviours
must make behaviour readable to an audience
reflection_prompts: What behaviour made the personality clearest?
What rule made it too predictable or too random?

invention_cards.open_ended_challenge: Invent a broken prototype and a fair repair process for another team.
constraints: fault must be solvable with evidence
must provide a safe test path
reflection_prompts: Was the fault too hidden or too obvious?
What evidence should the repair team collect first?

invention_cards.open_ended_challenge: Invent a small public installation that invites interaction and survives repeated visitors.
constraints: must be usable without verbal instruction
must handle at least ten repeated interactions
reflection_prompts: What did visitors try first?
What would make the installation easier to maintain?

red_team_cases.schema_weakness: Works cleanly only if facilitators remember that reading the button and making the motor move remain separate primitive skills.
The classification is clear for a fixed vehicle recipe, but it becomes invention if the vehicle mission is open-ended.
The integration can absorb the overlap, but the number of required prerequisite skills rises quickly.
Resolved by the fixed-integration versus open-invention rule; card authors must state how much choice children have.
red_team_cases.schema_rule: Fixed challenge with named Movement, Structures and Control/Input prerequisites is Integration; open-ended arm invention would be an Invention card.

red_team_cases.schema_weakness: The fixed challenge classifies well, but hinge construction and servo motion need a tie-breaker when taught separately.
Classification is stable when the alarm rule is fixed; open-ended security exhibit variants should move to invention.
This is one of the cleaner overlaps because the main learning problem is sequencing powered outputs.
If the game rules are invented by children, the same prompt should probably become an invention card.
The puppet becomes hard to classify if expression, mechanism, and fabrication are all equally central.
Resolved for passive hinges; actuated hinge projects still need integration cards.
red_team_cases.schema_rule: Passive joint support, alignment and range are Structures; actuated travel is Movement; combining both is Integration.

red_team_cases.schema_weakness: Resolved by the supporting-family and Debugging/Testing primary-family tie-breaker; still needs a rationale field when used in curriculum.
red_team_cases.schema_rule: Diagnostic method primary; circuit and power knowledge are supporting context.

red_team_cases.schema_weakness: Resolved for fixed chassis-improvement lessons; open-ended redesign challenges still belong as integrations or inventions.
red_team_cases.schema_rule: Improve stays in the family whose object is being improved; chassis strength is a Structures problem with debugging/design support.

red_team_cases.schema_weakness: Fits Power if battery capability is primary, but the schema has no explicit Safety support tag.
The prompt is invention when player rules are open-ended, but a fixed radio tag recipe is an integration.

audit_warnings.summary: Comfort, feedback, aesthetics, repairability and planning remain intent-sensitive and should keep explicit rationales when used as power cards.
Validation metrics are useful but should not be treated as proof that all ambiguity disappeared.
